summary: Using the provided preflight artifact as live state, #53698 remains the best canonical item for the narrow still-open bug: the WhatsApp web idle watchdog/message timeout is hardcoded at 30 minutes and not configurable on current main (295bcde7b82cf31e4dc1d14c110a288b4c60575a). No merge-ready canonical PR exists: #63939 is open but failing checks, #71466 is draft with skipped validation, and #67816 is security-sensitive and out of scope. I kept broader WhatsApp stability reports open as related or independent, blocked duplicate closeout for the two clearest duplicates because require_fix_before_close is set, routed the linked security-sensitive PR, and emitted a narrow fix artifact/open-fix plan for a fresh replacement PR.

actions.reason: Best surviving live canonical for the still-open idle-timeout configurability bug.
Bug remains real on current main and needs a fresh narrow fix path.
Replacement fix path is clearer than trying to merge current candidate PRs from incomplete/failing state.
Implementation/open-PR step is blocked by unavailable writable checkout, not by classification ambiguity.
Clear duplicate, but closeout is blocked until the fix-first gate is satisfied.
Clear duplicate of the canonical idle-watchdog bug, but closeout is blocked by the fix-first policy.
Overlaps the same stability area but carries broader auth/listener failure modes beyond the canonical timeout-config bug.
Same family, but not narrow enough to collapse into the canonical config-only bug without a landed fix path.
Broader logout/recovery bug in the same subsystem, not a pure duplicate of the idle-timeout issue.
Separate platform/network bug; should stay out of this canonical closeout path.
Startup/network timeout issue is distinct from the canonical idle-watchdog bug.
Related silent-delivery failure in the same area, but not a clean duplicate of the configurable-timeout request.
Distinct connection-open failure; not part of the canonical closeout path.
Same operational neighborhood, different remaining scope.
Likely follow-up cluster in the same subsystem, but not the same root cause.
Distinct health-monitor race regression; should stay open independently.
Related WhatsApp stability issue with overlapping symptoms, but not a safe duplicate.
Useful candidate/source PR, but not merge-ready and not safe as the canonical path in this run.
Relevant draft fix direction, but still too incomplete to use as canonical or to close issues against.
Out-of-scope security-sensitive PR; quarantine only this item and continue non-security classification.
